Finding_Summary: KRA set an initial approved budget of 2.39B for Road Maintenance Levy commissions.
Actual comparable collected commission for the Road Maintenance Levy was 2.35B.
KRA recorded a performance difference (shortfall) of 44.9M against the budget for RMLF commissions.
RMLF commission income rose significantly due to the review of the RML rate from Kshs 18 to Kshs 25 per litre of fuel.
KRA set an approved budget of 357.1M for Housing Levy commissions.
Actual comparable collected commission for the Housing Levy outperformed targets at 425M.
Housing Levy commission collections exceeded the comparative performance baseline by 70.9M.

Project_or_Specific_Issue: National Housing Development Levy Commission Income
Finding_Summary: Housing Levy commission recognized as income was 397.2M; a drop attributed to the reduction of the commission rate from 2% to 0.5%.
KRA was targeted to remit 2.347 Trillion to the Exchequer for national distribution.
KRA actually remitted 2.323 Trillion to the Exchequer representing a 99% performance rate.
KRA missed its overarching Exchequer remittance target by 24 Billion.
The target for Agency Revenue (funds collected on behalf of other entities) was 207.8 Billion.
KRA significantly overperformed on Agency Revenue collections netting 248.3 Billion.
KRA exceeded its target for Agency Revenues by 40.5 Billion.
The Auditor-General flagged 23.7 million lost excise stamps at the Times Tower vault leading to a potential revenue loss of 120.2M.
The majority of the lost excise stamps were for water resulting in an estimated 95.8M loss.

The Auditor-General reported a 2.39 Billion unexplained variance between agency income and actual Treasury funding received.
KRA failed to provide supporting details for insurance premiums amounting to 403.9M.

Project_or_Specific_Issue: Horn of Africa Gateway Development Project
